content: Clean uninstall is a known pain point users actively complain about: leftover folders in AppData/Library/Application Support, registry entries (Windows), preference plists (mac), and caches are the norm for careless uninstallers. Best practice: uninstaller should remove every file/folder/registry-key/plist it created, close-before-uninstall guards, and a manifest/log of installed paths so uninstall is deterministic rather than guessed.
